metadata.title: Seven Elements — Lodge7 | A Place to Touch Seven Elements of Nature
metadata.description: A private retreat at the foot of Aso-Kuju National Park in Taketa, Oita. An agricultural warehouse reborn where seven natural elements converge.
metadata.ogTitle: Seven Elements — Lodge7 | A Place to Touch Seven Elements of Nature
metadata.ogDescription: A converted agricultural warehouse in rural Oita, Japan. A private retreat where nature and architecture intertwine.

hero.title:
A farmhouse hideaway
where you touch
the rhythm of the earth.
hero.location: At the foot of Aso-Kuju National Park, Kuju Highlands, Taketa, Oita.
hero.description: A private retreat in a renovated agricultural warehouse, where seven elements of nature await.

hideout.label: The Hideout
hideout.title: A hideaway born from a farmhouse.
hideout.description: A one-group-per-day retreat in a renovated agricultural warehouse on the Kuju Highlands. Soaring ceilings, industrial steel, and natural materials converge in a space where silence and nature's rhythm replace the noise of the city.

elements.label: Seven Elements
elements.title: A place to touch seven elements of nature.
elements.sectionSubtitle: A place to touch seven elements of nature.

fire.name: Fire
fire.description: The flickering flames of a wood stove and bonfire. Their warmth envelops winter nights, creating moments that need no words.
fire.detailDescription: The Kuju mountain range is living land where volcanic activity continues to this day. When you light the wood stove at Lodge7, you join a dialogue with fire that stretches back to ancient times.

water.name: Water
water.description: Carbonated spring water, morning dewdrops. The sound of water is the rhythm of this land itself.
water.detailDescription: Taketa is known as the City of Water. With over fifty natural spring groups, crystal-clear water filtered through Aso's volcanic layers surfaces everywhere you look.

wind.name: Wind
wind.description: The breeze descending from the Kuju mountains. It is always the wind that first announces the turning of the seasons.
wind.detailDescription: The Kuju Highlands sit at over 600 meters above sea level — an open, expansive land. With nothing to block its path, the wind itself becomes the messenger of the seasons.

tree.name: Tree
tree.description: The grain of ceiling beams, the scent of forest trees. Wood is both the skeleton and the breath of this space.
tree.detailDescription: Step into Lodge7 and the first thing you notice is the thick beams spanning the ceiling. Each line of grain lends warmth and story to this building.

earth.name: Earth
earth.description: Agricultural land, the texture of soil beneath your feet. Here, you can feel the pulse of the earth directly.
earth.detailDescription: The land where Lodge7 stands sits upon a pyroclastic plateau born from Aso's great eruptions. Over tens of thousands of years, volcanic ash became rich soil.

thunder.name: Electricity
thunder.description: Using renewable energy, local residents generate the electricity that lights this lodge.
thunder.detailDescription: Across the grounds of Lodge7, 544kW of solar panels spread out under the highland sky. The Kuju Highlands enjoy exceptional sunlight conditions.

light.name: Light
light.description: The moment morning light spills into the warehouse. The golden hour at dusk. And a sky full of stars. In this place, light marks the passage of time.
light.detailDescription: At Lodge7, light takes the place of a clock. Wake to the first rays entering through the east window. Spend the day bathed in natural light from the high ceiling.

wonder.text1: Within walking distance of Lodge7, there is nothing but a hot spring.
wonder.text2: It is, without question, an inconvenient place.
wonder.text3: But here, you'll find the genuine life of a farming village.
wonder.text4: And if you stand here and look up at the sky—
wonder.emphasis:
you will surely sense the quiet power
that this world holds.

space.label: The Space
space.title: Where industrial meets nature.
space.description: Soaring ceilings, exposed steel beams, and the warmth of wood. A space that preserves the bones of the original warehouse while finishing with natural materials — offering a tranquility no city hotel can match.

coffee.label: Specialty Coffee
coffee.name: Mikazuki Coffee Roasters
coffee.description:
Enjoy beans from Mikazuki Coffee Roasters (Geshorō),
a beloved café in Taketa city.
Wake to the luxury of freshly ground coffee on a Kuju Highland morning.

items.name: Wood Stove / Fire Pit
items.desc: An authentic wood stove and fire pit for cozy winter nights

items.name: Coffee
items.desc:
Mikazuki Coffee Roasters beans
Hand drip equipment provided

items.name: Wi-Fi
items.desc: Starlink installed. Connected directly to space.

items.name: Towels
items.desc: Bath towels and face towels provided

cards.title: Joining the rhythm of the land
cards.description: Agriculture is the heart of this land. We honor the lives of local farmers and exist as part of their daily rhythm.

cards.title: Preserving the silence
cards.description: The nights on the Kuju Highlands are remarkably quiet. This silence is a precious treasure that the community and nature have protected for generations.

cards.title: Part of the community
cards.description: Lodge7 is not a tourist facility — it is a member of the local community. Through your stay, we invite you to become part of this land's story.

location.label: Location
location.title: At the foot of Aso-Kuju National Park.
location.description: Kuju Highlands, Taketa, Oita Prefecture. A land of silence and starlit skies, embraced by Aso-Kuju National Park.
location.nearby:
• Shichirida Onsen (4 min walk)
• Gansey Farm (10 min drive)
• Kuju Winery (12 min drive)
• Nagayu Onsen (5 min drive)
• Oka Castle Ruins (30 min drive)
location.accessText:
From Oita Airport: approx. 90 min by car
From Kumamoto Airport: approx. 80 min by car
From Oita/Beppu Station: approx. 60 min by car
From Kumamoto City: approx. 110 min by car
From Fukuoka City: approx. 150 min by car
location.accessNote: * Detailed directions will be sent by the host after booking on Airbnb.

spots.name: Taketa Castle Town & Oka Castle Ruins

footerCta.title:
A special time awaits
in the farmhouse hideaway.

lodge7.heroTitle: In a farmhouse hideaway, touch the rhythm of the earth.
lodge7.description:
Lodge7 is the first property of Seven Elements.

Standing in the rural landscape of Taketa, Oita, this former agricultural warehouse has been transformed into a modern, comfortable private retreat.
lodge7.amenities:
• Wood stove / Fire pit
• EV charging (Tesla Wall Connector)
• Coffee (Mikazuki Coffee Roasters)
• Wi-Fi (Starlink)
• Kitchenette
• BBQ grill

about.heroTitle: A philosophy rooted in the elements of nature
about.story:
Seven Elements began with a single question.

"Can we create a place where nature and human life intersect once more?"

Taketa, Oita Prefecture. A rural landscape at the foot of Aso-Kuju National Park. Here, seven natural elements still breathe within everyday life.
about.mission: Listen to the elements. Walk alongside the working earth. That is the philosophy of Seven Elements.

contact.description: Have a question or want to learn more? We'd love to hear from you.

form.name: Name
form.thankYou:
Thank you for reaching out.
We'll get back to you soon.
